body.title: pe_chain OBSERVE window collapses near file boundaries — relevant code falls outside slice
body.result: Bumped _OBSERVE_CONTEXT_LINES 40→150, _OBSERVE_MAX_SECTION 120→300, added _OBSERVE_FULL_FILE_THRESHOLD=300 (small files read whole), and _read_file_section rebalances when one edge clips a file boundary. Cert walk-02 dump confirms HYPOTHESIZE now anchors on real constants instead of docstrings.
body.description:
Cert walk-02 (T-consult-confidence-threshold-raise) attempt 1 exposed an OBSERVE bug. _grep_file picked first match (line 13 of consult.py — docstring); _read_file_section centered window on line 13 with ±40 line context; max(0, 13-41) clipped lower edge to 0 but did not extend the upper edge to compensate. Result: window covered lines 1-53 but the actual constant DEFAULT_MIN_CONFIDENCE = 0.55 lives at line 70 — outside the slice. HYPOTHESIZE saw only the docstring + imports, hallucinated `"confidence": 0.62` from ticket text rather than reading actual constant.

Fix shipped uncommitted in this branch:
- _OBSERVE_CONTEXT_LINES bumped 40 → 150
- _OBSERVE_MAX_SECTION bumped 120 → 300
- _OBSERVE_FULL_FILE_THRESHOLD added (300 lines): files at/below threshold read whole
- _read_file_section rebalances window when one edge clips a file boundary (shifts the unclipped edge to keep budget)

Evidence: cert_walk_02_attempt_1_observe_miss.json (lost to overwrite — captured the symptom in the cert walk report already) and cert_walk_02_attempt_2_regressive_edit.json (preserved at ~/.TheIgors/local/logs/) shows the fix correctly anchors hypothesis at the real DEFAULT_MIN_CONFIDENCE constant, exposing a second engram bug (regressive-edit direction) for separate investigation.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (_OBSERVE_* constants + _read_file_section)
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — OBSERVE window heuristic. OUT — HYPOTHESIZE prompt, multi-range observation, large-file (>300 line) handling beyond budget+rebalance.
**Test plan:** existing 91 pe_chain tests pass with new constants. Cert walk-02 attempt 2 is the live integration test — confirms HYPOTHESIZE now reads actual constant.
